_doc: Reliability manifest for the MCP fleet — consumed by `mcp-sync verify` and per-repo deploy/preflight.sh. For each server: whether a loop that lists it should treat it as REQUIRED (fail loud / exit non-zero at preflight) or optional (record degraded, continue); the credential it needs and where that cred lives; the token type; and the cheap auth probe to run at startup. Server *definitions* live in mcp.json; this file is the operational contract. See the deep-research report (2026-05-30) for rationale.

slack-dm.scope_source: x-oauth-scopes response header on any Web API call (NOT auth.test body)
slack-dm.preflight: auth.test -> ok:true AND assert required_scopes subset of x-oauth-scopes header
slack-dm.notes: Read path uses conversations.history (im:history) + inline reactions (reactions:read). NEVER search.messages (user-token/search:read only). Scope changes require an app REINSTALL (human). Reactions returned inline; reactions.get(full=true) only for the complete reactor list.

github.token_type: fine-grained PAT (ghp_/github_pat_) — NOT a gh CLI gho_ OAuth token, NOT a ghs_ App installation token
github.preflight: GET /rate_limit or get_me with the Bearer PAT -> 200
github.notes: gho_ failed with 401: the remote MCP validates token audience and a gh-CLI OAuth user token has the wrong audience. Remote endpoint accepts only (1) one-click OAuth or (2) a PAT. Headless/CI loops should OMIT this server and use the `gh` CLI (vizcom-sre, vizcom-review) instead of shipping a server that can't auth unattended.

datadog.preflight: any read with both header keys -> 200
datadog.notes: US5. Use ?toolsets= to limit exposed tools (already core,apm,error-tracking,software-delivery).

sentry.token_type: Sentry user auth token (org:read project:read event:read member:read)
sentry.notes: Remote OAuth server. Headless: pre-mint the auth token once and store in the secrets env file; do NOT rely on interactive OAuth in cron/systemd. If absent, strict-config it out rather than hang startup.

linear.notes: Remote OAuth — read-only use. Headless agents that can't complete OAuth should omit it from their strict config (it was a startup-hang contributor).

npx-stdio.notes: npx-launched stdio servers need node and a (possibly cold) network fetch on first run — keep them OUT of headless/VM strict configs (they contributed to the claude -p first-turn hang). Versions are pinned in mcp.json; run `mcp-sync drift` in CI.

preflight_policy.required_fail: exit non-zero with a named per-server/per-scope error so the runner alerts; do not start the tick.
preflight_policy.optional_fail: record a degraded-capability line in the tick output (never claim success for a down capability) and continue.
preflight_policy.strict_config_caveat: --strict-mcp-config does NOT override disabledMcpServers in ~/.claude.json (Claude Code bug #14490); verify must catch a required server disabled there. Always pass a non-empty --mcp-config with strict or it silently strips all servers.
